nodes.label: Password auth enabled
nodes.description: SSH password auth allows brute force. Disable: PasswordAuthentication no

nodes.label: Root SSH login permitted
nodes.description: PermitRootLogin yes — direct root access, no audit trail

nodes.label: Weak SSH cipher
nodes.description: diffie-hellman-group1-sha1 present — Logjam attack vector

nodes.label: No SSH idle timeout
nodes.description: Abandoned sessions usable for lateral movement

nodes.label: Unlimited SSH auth attempts
nodes.description: Enables slow brute force undetected by default fail2ban rules

nodes.label: X11 forwarding enabled
nodes.description: Creates local socket exploitable for privilege escalation

nodes.label: Agent forwarding enabled
nodes.description: SSH agent hijacking in multi-hop scenarios

nodes.label: Container running as root
nodes.description: Root in container = root on host if escape. USER directive missing.

nodes.label: No container resource limits
nodes.description: Single container can starve all others — DoS via resource exhaustion

nodes.label: Container in privileged mode
nodes.description: Full host kernel access — complete container escape

nodes.label: Mutable container filesystem
nodes.description: Attacker can persist changes inside container across restarts

nodes.label: Secrets passed via ENV vars
nodes.description: docker inspect exposes ENV secrets to any process on host

nodes.label: Secrets in Dockerfile ARG
nodes.description: Build args visible in image history: docker history <image>

nodes.label: Unpinned Docker base image
nodes.description: FROM node:latest — silent image swap introduces CVEs

nodes.label: No image signature verification
nodes.description: No guarantee image hasn't been tampered with in registry

nodes.label: Default bridge network
nodes.description: All containers on default bridge can communicate without restriction

nodes.label: Action pinned to floating tag
nodes.description: uses: action@v2 — tag can be moved to malicious commit (tj-actions attack)

nodes.label: Unsafe pull_request_target
nodes.description: pull_request_target + fork checkout = attacker code with write permissions

nodes.label: Script injection via context
nodes.description: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }} in run: step = shell injection

nodes.label: GITHUB_TOKEN over-permissioned
nodes.description: No permissions: block — default is broad read-write

nodes.label: Secrets visible in CI logs
nodes.description: printenv or debug logging exposes all secrets to log readers

nodes.label: Unpinned npm install in CI
nodes.description: npm install without lockfile = supply chain attack surface

nodes.label: Slopsquatting risk
nodes.description: AI hallucinates package names. Attacker registers the hallucinated name.

nodes.label: Secret sprawl in AI code
nodes.description: AI-generated code hardcodes secrets 2x more than human code

nodes.label: Zombie infrastructure
nodes.description: Vibe-deployed infra forgotten — no one owns it, no one patches it

nodes.label: Silent DB schema drift
nodes.description: AI migration runs clean but corrupts downstream reports 3 months later

nodes.label: Hallucinated dependencies
nodes.description: AI suggests package that doesn't exist — real package name squatted

nodes.label: No MFA on admin accounts
nodes.description: Single factor auth — credential stuffing and phishing bypass

nodes.label: Weak session token entropy
nodes.description: Predictable session IDs enable session fixation attacks

nodes.label: No login rate limiting
nodes.description: Credential stuffing attacks go undetected

nodes.label: JWT none algorithm accepted
nodes.description: Unsigned JWT accepted as valid — complete auth bypass

nodes.label: Public S3 bucket
nodes.description: Misconfigured bucket exposes data to anyone on internet

nodes.label: Unencrypted storage at rest
nodes.description: Data readable if disk is physically or logically accessed

nodes.label: No VPC network isolation
nodes.description: Services reachable from internet that should be internal-only

nodes.label: Default service credentials
nodes.description: Default admin:admin or similar — first thing attackers try

nodes.label: Missing audit logs
nodes.description: Breach undetectable — no forensic trail

nodes.label: PasswordAuthentication no
nodes.description: Set in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

nodes.label: Pin action to commit SHA
nodes.description: uses: owner/repo@<full-sha> # tag

nodes.label: Use secrets manager (not ENV)
nodes.description: AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, or GitHub Secrets — never ENV vars in Dockerfile

nodes.label: Add Docker resource limits
nodes.description: --memory=512m --cpus=0.5 or deploy.resources in compose

nodes.label: Run container as non-root USER
nodes.description: USER 1001 in Dockerfile, verify with docker run --user

nodes.label: Pin base image to digest
nodes.description: FROM node:20@sha256:<digest> — immutable reference

nodes.label: Use env: for untrusted input
nodes.description: Set env: PR_TITLE: ${{ ... }} then use $PR_TITLE in shell

nodes.label: Add permissions: block
nodes.description: permissions: contents: read — minimum needed per job

nodes.label: Enable fail2ban for SSH
nodes.description: Ban IP after 3 failed attempts — blocks slow brute force

nodes.label: Commit package lockfile, use ci
nodes.description: npm ci not npm install — uses exact lockfile versions

nodes.label: tj-actions supply chain compromise
nodes.description: March 2025: tj-actions/changed-files compromised, 23,000+ repos leaked secrets via CI logs

nodes.label: Codecov bash uploader backdoor
nodes.description: April 2021: Codecov's bash uploader modified to exfiltrate CI environment variables

nodes.label: Uber HackerOne MFA fatigue
nodes.description: September 2022: Attacker spammed MFA push notifications until employee approved

nodes.label: SolarWinds Orion supply chain
nodes.description: 2020: Build pipeline compromised, malicious code shipped in signed update to 18,000 customers

nodes.label: Log4Shell remote code execution
nodes.description: December 2021: CVE-2021-44228, JNDI lookup in log messages, billions of Java apps affected

nodes.label: Capital One SSRF + S3 misconfiguration
nodes.description: 2019: SSRF on EC2 metadata endpoint + public S3 bucket, 100M records exposed

nodes.label: event-stream malicious dependency
nodes.description: 2018: npm package (8M weekly downloads) compromised via maintainer handoff, stole Bitcoin wallets
